title: UK Considers Social Media Ban for Under-16s Amid Safety Concerns
subtitle: Parents and politicians push for stronger rules to protect young people online.

summary.en: The UK government is looking at new rules for social media use by children, including a possible ban for those under 16, after a public discussion period ended. This move comes as many parents, whose children have been harmed or died due to online content, are asking for urgent action. While some support a complete ban, others suggest a more detailed approach, like blocking harmful features or only allowing access to safe platforms. Former government officials and campaigners believe tech companies design their products to be addictive and harmful to young people. The government has promised to introduce new measures by the end of 2026, aiming to protect children from online dangers.
summary.zh: 英國政府在公開諮詢期結束後，正在研究針對兒童使用社群媒體的新規定，包括可能禁止未滿 16 歲者使用。此舉是因許多父母的孩子因網路內容而受害或死亡，他們要求政府緊急採取行動。雖然有些人支持全面禁令，但其他人則建議採取更詳細的方法，例如阻止有害功能或僅允許使用安全平台。前政府官員和倡議者認為，科技公司設計其產品是為了讓年輕人上癮並造成傷害。政府已承諾在 2026 年底前推出新措施，旨在保護兒童免受網路危險。

paragraphs.en: The UK government is currently considering strong new rules for how children use social media, including a possible ban for those under 16. This discussion comes after a public consultation, which is a process where the government asks for opinions from the public, recently closed. Many families and campaigners are urging the government to act quickly, saying that social media has caused serious harm, and even death, to young people.
paragraphs.zh: 英國政府目前正在考慮針對兒童使用社群媒體制定嚴格的新規定，其中可能包括禁止未滿 16 歲者使用。這項討論是在最近結束的公開諮詢之後進行的，公開諮詢是政府徵求公眾意見的過程。許多家庭和倡議者正敦促政府迅速採取行動，表示社群媒體已對年輕人造成嚴重傷害，甚至導致死亡。

paragraphs.en: Sir Keir Starmer, the Prime Minister, has promised to take "decisive" action after meeting with parents who believe their children died because of social media use. According to BBC News, Sir Keir said it was important to act and that any action would be a "game-changer." However, some parents are not fully confident that the government will take strong enough steps. Ellen Roome, whose 14-year-old son Jools Sweeney died in 2022, told the BBC that she has heard promises before and remains "sceptical" until she sees real change. Mariano Janin, whose daughter Mia took her own life at 14 in 2021 after online bullying, also told the BBC he would "like to believe" action will be taken, but noted that the situation remains the "same status quo" with tech companies quickly releasing new products, like AI chatbots.
paragraphs.zh: 首相凱爾·史塔默 (Sir Keir Starmer) 在會見了認為孩子因使用社群媒體而喪生的父母後，承諾將採取「果斷」行動。根據 BBC 新聞報導，史塔默爵士表示採取行動至關重要，任何行動都將是「遊戲規則的改變者」。然而，一些父母對政府是否會採取足夠強硬的措施並不完全有信心。艾倫·魯姆 (Ellen Roome) 的 14 歲兒子朱爾斯·史威尼 (Jools Sweeney) 於 2022 年去世，她告訴 BBC，她以前聽過承諾，在看到真正的改變之前仍持「懷疑」態度。瑪麗亞諾·賈寧 (Mariano Janin) 的女兒米亞 (Mia) 於 2021 年因網路霸凌在 14 歲時輕生，他也告訴 BBC，他「希望相信」會採取行動，但指出情況仍是「現狀」，科技公司迅速推出新產品，例如人工智慧聊天機器人。

paragraphs.en: Campaigners are broadly divided on whether a complete ban on social media apps for children is the best way forward. The Guardian Technology reports that some ministers have already pledged to introduce an Australia-style ban for under-16s or to restrict "addictive" features like infinite scrolling. Since March, the government has been asking parents and children about measures such as app curfews and stronger age checks, even trying these out in some UK homes.
paragraphs.zh: 倡議者對於全面禁止兒童使用社群媒體應用程式是否是最佳前進方向，意見大致分歧。《衛報科技版》報導，一些部長已承諾針對未滿 16 歲者實施澳洲式的禁令，或限制「令人上癮」的功能，例如無限滾動。自三月以來，政府一直在向父母和兒童徵詢有關應用程式宵禁和更嚴格的年齡檢查等措施的意見，甚至在英國一些家庭中進行了試驗。

paragraphs.en: Liz Kendall, the Technology Secretary, stated to BBC News that the question is not whether the government will act, but that they "will." She added that new measures for under-16s are expected by the end of 2026, with a response to the consultation published in the summer. The government's review is looking at many different issues and features of social media.
paragraphs.zh: 科技大臣莉茲·肯德爾 (Liz Kendall) 向 BBC 新聞表示，問題不在於政府是否會採取行動，而是他們「將會」採取行動。她補充說，針對未滿 16 歲者的新措施預計將於 2026 年底前推出，而諮詢的回應將於夏季發布。政府的審查正在研究社群媒體的許多不同問題和功能。

paragraphs.en: Former health secretary Wes Streeting, who recently left Sir Keir Starmer's cabinet, has strongly called for a ban on social media for under-16s in the UK. According to BBC News, Streeting accused regulators and politicians of being "asleep at the wheel." He compared the tactics of big tech companies to those of big tobacco, saying they design platforms to be addictive and harmful to children. The Guardian Technology reported that Streeting believes there is "a growing body of evidence about the impact of this technology on childhood," affecting sleep, concentration, learning, health, and mental well-being. He emphasized that the "precautionary principle" should apply, meaning action should be taken quickly to prevent harm.
paragraphs.zh: 前衛生大臣韋斯·史翠汀 (Wes Streeting) 最近離開了凱爾·史塔默爵士的內閣，他強烈呼籲英國禁止未滿 16 歲者使用社群媒體。根據 BBC 新聞報導，史翠汀指責監管機構和政治人物「玩忽職守」。他將大型科技公司的策略比作大型菸草公司，稱他們設計平台是為了讓兒童上癮並造成傷害。《衛報科技版》報導，史翠汀認為「越來越多的證據表明這項技術對童年的影響」，影響睡眠、專注力、學習、健康和心理健康。他強調應適用「預防原則」，這意味著應迅速採取行動以防止傷害。

paragraphs.en: Streeting, now "liberated" to speak more freely, said he had pushed for stronger action while in government. He cited evidence from Australia, which has banned social media for under-16s, as proof that such measures can prevent harm, even if some children find ways around the ban. He called the slow approach to this issue "pretty shocking."
paragraphs.zh: 史翠汀現在「解放」了，可以更自由地發言，他說他在政府任職期間曾推動更強硬的行動。他引用澳洲的證據，該國已禁止未滿 16 歲者使用社群媒體，證明此類措施可以防止傷害，即使有些兒童會找到規避禁令的方法。他稱這種對此問題的緩慢處理方式「相當令人震驚」。

paragraphs.en: However, not everyone agrees on a full ban. Ian Russell, whose 14-year-old daughter Molly took her own life after seeing harmful content online, is a prominent online safety campaigner. According to The Guardian Technology, Russell believes a complete ban would create a "cliff edge," meaning teenagers would be suddenly exposed to harmful content on unchanged platforms once they turn 16. Instead, he supports a more detailed approach: blocking under-16s from platforms that do not meet strict safety rules, while allowing them to use "safe" apps. Russell also wants a ban on "aggressive algorithms" that show harmful content to teens and a focus on features like infinite scrolling and autoplay.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，並非所有人都同意全面禁令。伊恩·羅素 (Ian Russell) 的 14 歲女兒茉莉 (Molly) 在看到有害內容後輕生，他是一位著名的網路安全倡議者。根據《衛報科技版》報導，羅素認為全面禁令會造成「懸崖效應」，這意味著青少年一旦年滿 16 歲，就會突然接觸到未經改變的平台上的有害內容。相反，他支持更詳細的方法：禁止未滿 16 歲者使用不符合嚴格安全規則的平台，同時允許他們使用「安全」應用程式。羅素還希望禁止向青少年展示有害內容的「攻擊性演算法」，並關注無限滾動和自動播放等功能。

paragraphs.en: Esther Ghey, whose daughter Brianna was murdered in 2023, supports raising the age limit for social media. She believes social media addiction contributed to her daughter's mental health problems and led her to take risks with her safety. The Guardian Technology reports that Ghey, who founded the Brianna Ghey Legacy Project to promote online safety education, also wants limits on addictive features and more help for teens to understand the online world. She stressed that simply taking something away is not enough; children need to be taught "digital literacy" so they have the "emotional maturity" to deal with social media when they use it.
paragraphs.zh: 艾絲特·蓋伊 (Esther Ghey) 的女兒布莉安娜 (Brianna) 於 2023 年被謀殺，她支持提高社群媒體的年齡限制。她認為社群媒體成癮導致她女兒的心理健康問題，並使她冒險危及自身安全。《衛報科技版》報導，蓋伊創立了「布莉安娜·蓋伊遺產計畫」(Brianna Ghey Legacy Project) 以推廣網路安全教育，她還希望限制令人上癮的功能，並提供更多幫助讓青少年了解網路世界。她強調，僅僅拿走某些東西是不夠的；孩子們需要學習「數位素養」，這樣他們在使用社群媒體時才能具備「情感成熟度」來應對。

paragraphs.en: Young people themselves have different views. Fin, a 17-year-old student, told The Guardian Technology that the government's ideas to restrict social media for under-16s are "incredibly harsh." He noted that many young people use these platforms to follow news and stay in touch with friends. He suggested there could be different levels of restrictions instead of a complete ban, even if that might be more complicated.
paragraphs.zh: 年輕人自己也有不同的看法。17 歲的學生芬恩 (Fin) 告訴《衛報科技版》，政府限制未滿 16 歲者使用社群媒體的想法「非常嚴苛」。他指出，許多年輕人使用這些平台來追蹤新聞並與朋友保持聯繫。他建議可以有不同程度的限制，而不是全面禁令，即使這可能更複雜。

paragraphs.en: As the government prepares its response to the consultation, the debate continues on how best to protect children in an increasingly digital world. The aim is to ensure that legislation and protection keep up with changing technology to safeguard future generations. The current minimum age for most major social media platforms in the UK is 13, set by data protection rules, but there is no formal legal limit for social media use itself.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著政府準備對諮詢做出回應，關於如何在日益數位化的世界中最好地保護兒童的辯論仍在繼續。目標是確保立法和保護措施與不斷變化的技術同步，以保護後代。目前英國大多數主要社群媒體平台的最低年齡為 13 歲，由資料保護規則設定，但社群媒體使用本身沒有正式的法律限制。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Something that completely changes the way a situation develops.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 徹底改變局勢發展的事物；顛覆者。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞指能帶來重大改變的事物。文中：According to BBC News, Sir Keir said it was important to act and that any action would be a "game-changer."

keyTerms.definitionEn: The existing state of affairs, especially regarding social or political issues.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 現狀，尤指社會或政治方面的現狀。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此拉丁文片語指維持現有的狀態。文中：Mariano Janin, whose daughter Mia took her own life at 14 in 2021 after online bullying, also told the BBC he would "like to believe" action will be taken, but noted that the situation remains the "same status quo" with tech companies quickly releasing new products, like AI chatbots.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Not paying attention to one's responsibilities or to a developing situation.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 疏忽職守；對正在發展的局勢不夠警覺。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此慣用語比喻駕駛時睡著，引申為未能盡責或對情況不夠警覺。文中：According to BBC News, Streeting accused regulators and politicians of being "asleep at the wheel."

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation that is very dangerous and where there is a risk of something bad happening very soon.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 危險的邊緣；即將面臨嚴重後果的局面。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語比喻懸崖邊緣，指危險且可能很快發生壞事的局面。文中：According to The Guardian Technology, Russell believes a complete ban would create a "cliff edge," meaning teenagers would be suddenly exposed to harmful content on unchanged platforms once they turn 16.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Starmer vows to act on social media after meeting bereaved parents

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Mother of boy who may have died in TikTok challenge urges No 10 to ban social media

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: ‘A tsunami of harm’: views on tackling online safety for under-16s in the UK

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the current minimum age for most major social media platforms in the UK, as set by data protection rules?

options.D: There is no formal legal limit set by data protection rules.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 B。文章的最後一段明確指出：「The current minimum age for most major social media platforms in the UK is 13, set by data protection rules」。這表示英國大多數主要社群媒體平台目前的最低年齡限制是 13 歲，這是由資料保護規定所設定的。選項 A、C 和 D 都與文章中明確敘述的事實不符。選項 A (16歲) 是政府考慮的可能禁令年齡，而非現行規定。選項 C (14歲) 是文中提及一些受害者或支持者子女的年齡，與現行規定無關。選項 D 則直接與文章內容矛盾。

quiz.question: Based on the various opinions presented, what can be inferred about the UK government's final decision regarding social media regulation for under-16s?

options.A: The government is highly likely to implement a complete ban on social media for all under-16s.
options.B: The government will probably decide against any new regulations due to divided public opinion.
options.C: The final regulations are likely to be a nuanced approach, potentially combining age restrictions with feature limitations and digital literacy education.
options.D: The government will prioritize the views of tech companies and allow them to self-regulate.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 C。文章中提到政府正在考慮多種措施，包括「Australia-style ban for under-16s or to restrict 'addictive' features like infinite scrolling」（第三段），以及「app curfews and stronger age checks」（第三段）。此外，Ian Russell 提倡「blocking under-16s from platforms that do not meet strict safety rules, while allowing them to use 'safe' apps」並「ban on 'aggressive algorithms'」（第七段）。Esther Ghey 則強調「digital literacy」和「emotional maturity」（第八段）。這些多元的討論表明政府最終的決策很可能是一個綜合性的方案，而非單一的全面禁令，因此選項 C 最能反映這種推論。選項 A 雖然有部分支持者，但文章也提到「not everyone agrees on a full ban」（第七段），且有其他替代方案被討論，所以全面禁令並非唯一或最確定的結果。選項 B 和 D 與文章中政府「will act」的承諾（第四段）以及各方對加強監管的呼籲相悖。

quiz.question: In the sentence, "Ian Russell, whose 14-year-old daughter Molly took her own life after seeing harmful content online, is a prominent online safety campaigner. According to The Guardian Technology, Russell believes a complete ban would create a 'cliff edge,' meaning teenagers would be suddenly exposed to harmful content on unchanged platforms once they turn 16." What does the phrase "cliff edge" most closely mean in this context?

options.A: A sudden, dangerous, and unprepared transition
options.B: A clear and distinct boundary or limit
options.C: A high point from which to view the landscape

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 A。在文章中，「cliff edge」被解釋為「meaning teenagers would be suddenly exposed to harmful content on unchanged platforms once they turn 16」。這表示 Russell 擔心如果實施全面禁令，青少年在 16 歲時會突然且毫無準備地面臨所有有害內容，這是一個危險且沒有緩衝的轉變。因此，選項 A「A sudden, dangerous, and unprepared transition」最符合其語境意義。選項 B 雖然「cliff edge」可以指邊界，但在這裡強調的是「突然」和「危險」的後果，而非單純的界線。選項 C 和 D 則完全偏離了這個詞在文章中的負面和警告意味。

quiz.question: What is the main idea of the article?

options.A: The UK government has already decided to ban social media for all children under 16.
options.B: Social media companies are successfully resisting all attempts at government regulation in the UK.
options.C: Parents and campaigners are unified in their demand for a complete ban on social media for young people.
options.D: The UK government is actively considering various strong new regulations for children's social media use, facing diverse opinions on the best approach to protect young people from harm.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 D。文章的核心內容是英國政府正在審議針對兒童社群媒體使用的新規定（第一段），並探討了包括全面禁令、限制成癮功能、加強年齡驗證和數位素養教育等各種提議。文章也呈現了各方對這些措施的不同意見，例如 Wes Streeting 支持全面禁令，而 Ian Russell 則擔心「cliff edge」效應，並提倡更細緻的監管，年輕人 Fin 也認為全面禁令過於嚴苛。這表明政府正在積極考慮，且面臨多樣化的意見。選項 A 不對，因為文章指出政府仍在「considering」階段，尚未做出最終決定。選項 B 不對，因為文章明確表示政府「will act」（第四段），且正在進行公眾諮詢和審查。選項 C 不對，因為文章清楚表明「Campaigners are broadly divided on whether a complete ban on social media apps for children is the best way forward」（第三段），且 Ian Russell 和 Esther Ghey 提出了更為多元的解決方案。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
